subject: Mathematics
title: Defining the Golden Ratio
description: A fast breakdown of Defining the Golden Ratio for builders.

hook.text: Cut a line so the long part over the short equals the whole over the long. You always get 1.618.

buildup.text: This special ratio is called phi. It's the positive solution to the equation x squared = x + 1.

discovery.text: Phi is irrational—its decimal expansion never ends and never repeats, going on forever.

twist.text: Its continued fraction is all ones: 1 + 1/(1 + 1/(1 + ...)). It's the most 'irrational' irrational number.

climax.text: The golden ratio is self-similar: subtract 1 and you get 1/phi. It's its own reciprocal plus one.

punchline.text: One ratio that perfectly relates itself to itself.

quiz.question: What equation does the golden ratio satisfy?
